response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is a multiple of 3 because 0 divided by 3 is 0 so result[0] is calculated as the sum of squares of the differences between nums[0] and all other elements in the array

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[1] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 because 2 divided by 3 is 0 with remainder 2 so result[2] is calculated as the sum of squares of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array

for example in the first example nums is [2,3,5] the result array is [14,3,5] because result[0] is calculated as the sum of squares of the differences between 2 and 2, 2 and 3, and 2 and 5 which is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 but the example shows it as 10, result[1] is calculated as the sum of absolute differences between 3 and 2, 3 and 3, and 3 and 5 which is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3, and result[2] is calculated as the sum of squares of the differences between 5 and 2, 5 and 3, and 5 and 5 which is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

***
